summary: Accepted R2 after verifying the 1440x1000 horizontal overflow regression is fixed, the new Playwright no-horizontal-overflow test meaningfully covers the repro, scope remains within allowed paths, no new dependency drift exists, and no authority mutation or forbidden package boundary violation was found.

tests_run.result: Chromium Playwright passed 4 tests, including the new 1440x1000 M3 appointment no-horizontal-overflow measurement.
document scrollWidth and clientWidth were both 1440; M3 grid right edge was 1418; no M3 panel overlaps were detected.
Web build passed with the existing chunk-size warning; Prettier, ESLint, recursive typecheck, and architecture boundary check passed.

tests_run.command: scope, dependency, handoff, task, and authority scans
tests_run.result: Task and R2 handoff validated; changed files stayed within allowed paths; no forbidden paths, dependency drift, WorldState storage, Redux, Electron IPC, or forbidden package imports were found.

decisions: Accepted Playwright coverage because it directly asserts the prior QA repro condition at 1440x1000 instead of relying on screenshots or hidden overflow.
Did not write the handoff file locally because this review was explicitly read-only.
risks: The previously disclosed live-query integration risk remains: the M3 appointment workspace still uses protocol DTO projection fixtures until a future approved worker query adapter provides the page-level appointment read model.
The existing Vite chunk-size warning remains unrelated to this task.
requested_action: Lead orchestrator should validate this ACCEPT handoff JSON and proceed with task acceptance/integration routing.
